Then we have the close, right? We come to the end. So what do we have to do at the end? The most important thing is we have to tell what the offer is. What are we offering them? I think offers are a really overlooked way to increase response. You add more bonuses, it can increase response. You change the price, it can increase response. You change the way they pay. Maybe they pay every month, every other month, twice a week, whatever it is, every other Tuesday. That can increase response. Maybe the price has a 7 instead of a 9. That can increase response. So you've got your offer, which is the price. And one of the things you want to do with the price is you want to make the price seem inconsequential. You want to make the price seem less than it is. Or to them, it looks like less. And you do that by saying things like, oh, it's only a dollar a day for this newsletter. One euro a day. Oh, it's the same as a price of a cup of coffee. Imagine what you would pay if you went to a consultant and you asked for a consultant's advice. What if you went to a computer repair shop? They cost $60 an hour. And if you just went for one hour a month, that would be many times more than the price of your newsletter. What does it cost you when your computer isn't working? How valuable is that? To be able to rely on your computer for your business or for your home. How much is it worth to make $1 million in the stock market? Or to get 20% return on your money every year? $300, 300 euros is nothing to do that. It's nothing to be a better purchasing manager. It's nothing to be a better. So help them to understand that the price isn't really that expensive. It's really inconsequential. And so you've got the bonuses that you can add. Special reports to fill in gaps. If you've got a newsletter that's about a certain kind of investing, then maybe the bonuses could be about different kinds of investing. Maybe the bonuses could be about three stocks that are being overlooked. Maybe the bonuses could be about how to be a better investor, how to pay less in fees, how to buy stocks for the cheapest price, how not to get overcharged. So many things that you can do that don't really cost much money that you can add to make that 300 euros a year not seem like so much money. Maybe it's a free calculator. We had a lot of success with Boardroom with a free calculator. We did that for the tax product for a while. The idea was it could help you figure out how much money you're saving on taxes. Okay, so you got your offer and then you've got your guarantee. I promise you you'll get your money back or you won't be charged anything for 30 days. But even after you pay, I assume you'll give them the money back if they want their money back. So whatever you've got, if it's not that for every product, maybe you should try it being that for your product because a lot of times I think we're scared to guarantee things. We're scared to say, oh, lifetime guarantee. We're scared to say, we'll give you double your money back because my God, what if people all wanted their money back? What if people 80 years from now asked for a refund? But it doesn't happen. And when you look at the numbers, you see that by doing that, you may get slightly more returns, but you get a lot more money on the bottom line, a lot more profit, a lot more income, a lot more sales. So it's worth a try to see what happens. But whatever guarantee you have, you want to present it as strongly as possible. And one of the things is you never want to say it in a negative way. You never want to say, oh, if you hate my newsletter, we'll give you your money back. You want to say it positive. You want to say, you've got to love the newsletter. You've got to love my product. You've got to make a lot of money in the stock market. Your arthritis has to go away. You have to become a better purchasing agent. You have to become a better human resources director. You have to get that promotion you want. If you don't, then we'll happy to give you back your money. I'm exaggerating, but you could see how it gives you a chance to be positive and say positive things. And I think too many times, people say it in a negative way. If you're unhappy, we'll give you your money back. You also want the guarantee to come from your own confidence that they're going to be satisfied. You want the guarantee to come from... The reason I guarantee this so strongly, the reason I have a lifetime guarantee, the reason I have a full year guarantee, the reason I have whatever guarantee is because I know that this could work for you. That's why the guarantee is an expression of my confidence in this product. And make a big deal of the guarantee. Right, put it in a... Right, Laurie, sometimes you put it in a box. You put a nice... You said that, but I just opened up one of our biggest winning ones. You put a nice squiggly thing around it. It looks official. It's signed by the doctor, by the editor. It's got a photo of him, his personal signature. I guarantee you'll get your money back. Those things sound a little bit corny and hokey, but they really work because the more you can take the risk away. Right, no one wants risk. I don't want to buy something and subscribe to something and risk that I won't like it. But if you say to me, oh, I'm so... Your happiness is so important to me. If you don't like it, I insist you ask for your money back. Oh, now I feel comfortable spending a couple of hundred dollars on something. Because I know I can get it back if it doesn't work. People have a real fear of making mistakes. Nobody likes to look stupid. Nobody likes to do something and then go, oh, God, that was... Why did I subscribe to that newsletter? Why did I buy that new stereo? Why did I buy that new telephone or whatever? And so the more you can make it like there's no possibility for them to look stupid to make a mistake because they can undo it, right, then the sale is far more likely. So really emphasize the guarantee as much as you can. Then as you're getting toward the close scarcity, you can have someone really convinced they want your product, right? Really convinced your product is great. But if you don't convince them they have to act now, then they won't. You won't get the sale because they can always do it. They'll always put it off until tomorrow. So how do you do that? What is it that can make them act more quickly, right? You can give them a bonus for acting quickly, act within five days. But there's two things. One is time. Time is going to run out. This offer is only good for the next 30 days. This offer is only good until tomorrow. There's a limit to the time that they have to do this. So that impels them to act. The other is there's a limit to quantity. We've only got a certain number of these newsletters. We only allow a certain number of subscribers into our membership per day, right? We only allow a couple of hundred in per day. I sure hope there's an opening. When you apply, now the person thinks, oh, god, there might not be an opening. I've got to hurry up and apply to this. I've got to hurry up and do this. It works a lot better, of course, if it's a hard product, if there's a physical thing. We only print so many of these. We only make so many bottles of this supplement. This herb, we have to get it from the Amazon. And boy, I don't know when they might run out because there's a lot of unrest in the Amazon these days. All these things can make for scarcity. And they can be really real things. You can really make a real limit on how many people you let in. Maybe it is really hard to get this herb from the Amazon. Maybe you do get backordered sometimes. So the more you can build this in, the better. Because otherwise, people are very, a lot of inertia, people don't act, right? Anyone know what that is? It's an NLP term, Neuro-Linguistic Programming. And it means you take someone into the future. And the close is a really good place to do that. It's like, imagine. A lot of times, it'll start with imagine. Imagine what it'll be like when you open the newspaper every morning and you see that your stocks have gone up instead of down. Imagine what it'll be like to wake up in the morning and not to be reminded that you have arthritis because right away your knee hurts or your elbow hurts. Imagine what it'll be like to be able to come into your job as a human resource manager and not worry that you have to fire someone today. Because you know exactly what to do to let someone go that will not get you in trouble, will not have them come back and sue you, that you'll have the confidence you followed every law to the letter. Imagine what it'll be like to order raw materials and know that the price isn't going to go down right after you've ordered it and you're going to feel really bad. Imagine what it'll be like when you have four people around the globe looking for opportunities for you to invest in, hunting for opportunities, every corner of the world covered for you. Imagine what that will be like. The more you can get them to imagine what their bright future will be, the more you can paint that bright future, the more likely they're going to want your product which is going to make that future possible. And then crossroads. A lot of people use a clothes that's kind of like what's called a crossroads is, like you come to a crossroads, you're going down the road and then you're going down the road and you could go on this path like that or you could go on this path like that. So it's like a crossroads. So even if you don't use those exact words to be able to say to people, look, okay, I'm done. I've done everything I can to tell you about this opportunity. I've made this deal as fair as I possibly could. I've taken all the risk away from you. I've taken all the risk on my shoulders, not yours. I've made the price as low as I possibly can. I've made a special introductory price. I've done all these things. Now it's up to you. Now you're at a crossroads, right? And you have two choices. You can go down this road and you could continue to do things the way you've been doing them. You can continue to watch your stocks go down instead of up. You can continue to suffer from arthritis. You can continue to be frustrated as a purchasing agent. You can continue to be frustrated as a human resources person. You can continue with whatever frustration that's been in your life. Or now you have another choice. You could go down this road. You could buy our product. You could subscribe to our newsletter. And the life that I asked you to imagine a little while ago, that could be your life. You could have that change. You could have that transformation. Remember we said how important transformation is and change, right? It's an opportunity to say to them. You can have change. You could go down this road or you could continue down the road you've been going down. You have a choice. And it really kind of drives home to people that we're not just talking about whether I subscribe to this newsletter or not. It's not so simple. It's not just, should I subscribe? Should I not subscribe? That the choice is really, should I change my life or should I not change my life? Should I do something to make my life better or should I not do something to make my life better? So, that's what you want to kind of end with, is to put the responsibility on them for their lives, right? I've done everything I can. I've made it the best deal. I gave you all these free bonuses. I've made the price as low as I could. I've taken all the risk on my shoulders, taken it off your shoulders. I can't do anything else. It's your responsibility now to change your life. I can't want that for you more than you want it for you. You have to want that for you too. You have to take, now it's your turn. Just take the damn mouse and click on the button and buy the newsletter. That's all you have to do to have this different life. So you see, I've kind of gone on and on, but you can see the kind of passion that you need to put into that. It can't just be like, oh, please click the button below and we can get started receiving our newsletter every week. No, it's got to be more than that. This is a life changing moment now for you now that you've come to the end of this letter. I don't know that this is something you all do, but I found it's really effective is once you're done with everything, like the VSL, if you're doing a VSL, the VSL stopped and it's like 10 seconds, and then you say, if you're still here, or you can do the same thing in the letter. FAQ. Maybe you have some questions. So here are some questions that we typically get asked. And now you can spend some time just answering questions. And in the answering questions, you make that, it becomes a little mini sales pitch each time. Does this work on Tuesdays? Yes, it works every day of the week. Every day of the week, it will make it possible for you to invest in the stock market and make tons of money or fix your health or whatever. You always kind of loop back around to the sales message and just click here to get started. Will this work if I have rheumatoid arthritis? Yes, it works for rheumatoid arthritis. It works effectively because it does this by reducing inflammation and that means it works for both types. And that's why so many people use it around the world so successfully. To get your bottle, to get your copy of the newsletter, just click here. Use that. Use that frequently asked question thing. It's a very powerful thing to put even after you're done with it, even in print or on a VSL. What are some of the other things we talked about? Future pays them a little bit. Do a little crossroads thing at the end. Tell them what the offer is. Oh, by the way, there's one other thing I forgot to tell you. But you want to tell them exactly what to do and what's next. Like exactly what to do. Take your mouse, bring it to the red button below, click on it, and then here's what will happen. You will go to a page that looks like this and it will ask for this information. Because people have this incredible fear of the... Well first of all, people get very easily confused. So if you don't tell them exactly what to do, fill out the order form, put your name in address, go here, click here, do this. People will get very easily confused and you'll lose a lot of orders. And then people are also amazingly afraid of the unknown. Like, oh, when I click there, what will happen? Will it take all the money out of my credit card, my credit account? Will it charge me instantly? Will I have a chance to change my mind? All it's going to do is take you to a nice little page where we get some information from you and you'll have a chance to review your order. We'll tell you all about it. And then once you've done that in only six days to a week, your product will arrive on your doorstep and when it does, here's what I want you to do. I want you to try this. I want you to do this. Go to this section of the book first. Look at this. Do a really simple thing. Give them really step by step. What's going to happen? What's going to happen? So, tell your mom what the offer is, why the price is cheap. Don't bullshit your mother. You know what I mean? Don't lie to her. Don't try to con her. Don't feed her a lot of stuff that sounds like advertising. She'll just look at you and go, why do you sound like that? Just naturally, how you would talk. The color, it's guaranteed. The scarcity. And that's it. Let's just take an hour to see if we can put together a whole letter from beginning to end. Not 40-page thing, but just something that has a lot of the things that we've talked about just so we can have that experience.
